stories.title: Greece derails EU’s Russia sanctions plan
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries failed to reach a deal on sweeping new sanctions against Russia on Wednesday night, after Greece blocked the measures to protect its fuel shipping industry. Wednesday’s talks, which wrapped after 7.30 pm, were just the latest in a series of efforts to secure the unanimous agreement needed on the slew of energy and trade measures designed to drain Moscow’s war chest. The package was presented in June, but discussions over the details have dragged on, despite significant pa

stories.title: Hungary: Ex-PM Orban pledges resistance to Magyar 'tyranny'
stories.summary: Hungary's former prime minister alleges that his successor has "dismantled the rule of law and eliminated checks and balances." Peter Magyar has pursued Viktor Orban and his allies since winning April's elections.

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: Széklettranszplantációval kezelhetőnek tűnik a krónikus alvászavar

stories.title: Orbán: Örülök, hogy csak négyévente van világbajnokság, nem engedhetném meg magamnak ezt minden évben

stories.title: Puszta kézzel küzdött egy utas életéért egy magyar orvos a fedélzeten, a Ryanair szerint minden járaton van defibrillátor
stories.summary: Egy magyar kardiológus a kisgyerekével együtt utazott a légitársaság Madrid–Budapest járatán, amikor egy férfi rosszul lett. Az utast egy idő után újra kellett éleszteni. A légiutas-kísérők azt mondták az orvosnak, hogy semmilyen orvosi műszer nincs a fedélzeten. A Ryanair viszont azt írta a Telexnek, hogy minden gépükön van defibrillátor, de azt nem kérte az orvos.

stories.title: Why a 90-year-old treaty limits NATO in the Black Sea
stories.summary: A 90-year-old treaty still shapes the balance of power in the Black Sea. As Ukraine fights on against Russia, can NATO project power into waters it can't enter freely?

stories.summary: Brussels talks between senior diplomats now underway in attempt to sign off 21st package of sanctions, after previous failed attempts The Venice Biennale said it would challenge a European Union decision to withdraw a €2 ⁠million grant in response to the return of Russia to its 2026 edition, despite continuing aggression on Ukraine, Reuters reported. The Italian government sharply criticised the move, while the European Commission warned it ​would suspend or terminate funding if the Russian ‌pav

stories.title: Trump threatens to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for each strait of Hormuz attack – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Threat from US president comes after US military completes 11th night of strikes on Iran The Jordanian army said it intercepted four Iranian missiles on Wednesday with two additional missile falling in an open area, AFP reports. The two missiles that the army did not intercept fell in “two remote, uninhabited areas, without causing any human casualties or material damage” the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Poll: Trump’s MAGA base is souring on his Iran war
stories.summary: Half of President Donald Trump’s MAGA base thought Iran’s war was worth its economic costs in May. Now, just a little more than a third do. That precipitous, 13-point drop in just two months, according to  a new POLITICO Poll , reveals how the president’s most die-hard supporters are starting to agree with the majority of Americans who have long opposed the U.S.’ involvement in the war. Additionally, 37 percent of self-identified MAGA Trump voters say the U.S. should continue its invol

stories.title: Britain’s new defense chief Streeting says Israel has ‘fallen short’ in Gaza
stories.summary: FARNBOROUGH, ENGLAND — Israel has “fallen short” of Britain’s standards of conduct in Gaza and the West Bank, the U.K.’s new Defence Secretary Wes Streeting said Wednesday. Speaking to journalists at the Farnborough International Airshow in one of his first engagements in the job, Streeting said that as a “friend and ally,” Israel should be held to the same standard Britain holds itself to. “That is the standard that I think, I fear, they’ve fallen short of,” he added. The defe

stories.title: EU says Trump’s new generic drug tariffs won’t hit its exports
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission said Wednesday that it expects European generic medicines to be exempt from U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest tariffs, arguing that Washington committed to a zero tariff rate with last year’s transatlantic trade deal. Trump on Tuesday announced a plan to impose a 100 percent tariff on generic drugs starting in August 2028 as part of a bid to bring pharmaceutical production back to the United States. If implemented, the tariff would then jump to 200 percent i

stories.title: Trump threatens retaliatory strikes on key Iranian infrastructure as conflict escalates
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned that the United States would strike Iranian bridges and power plants if Iran continues to attack commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. In  a post on Truth Social , Trump proclaimed that any type of Iranian attack on vessels traveling through the waterway would trigger corresponding American strikes on key infrastructure, including locations in or around Tehran. “From this point forward, any time the Islamic Repu

stories.title: Trump administration steers $5B toward AI research
stories.summary: Federal agencies have committed more than $5 billion to an Energy Department-led effort to use artificial intelligence to accelerate scientific research, White House Office of Science and Technology Policy Director Michael Kratsios announced Wednesday. The funding marks one of the clearest signs yet of the Trump administration’s effort to reshape federal science spending, steering research dollars toward AI-powered projects and individual researchers rather than institutions such as un

stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan

stories.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine army chief after protests - The Latest
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired his commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in Ukraine’s biggest military shake-up in two years. Nationwide protests broke out after Zelenskyy sacked Mykhailo Fedorov as defence minister, who the Ukraine president says will now be brought back into government in a ‘prominent position’. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s senior international correspondent Luke Harding – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.summary: Mamdani admits he lacks power to arrest Israeli prime minister after saying he’s ‘not welcome’ in New York City Zohran Mamdani , the New York City mayor, doubled down on his support for the international criminal court (ICC) on Wednesday and called on federal authorities to execute the court’s warrant for Benjamin Netanyahu ’s arrest should he visit the city in the fall. “I call on the federal government to join the international criminal court and to execute both this warrant as well as any war

stories.title: OpenAI says its AI model went rogue and hacked startup
stories.summary: OpenAI called it an "unprecedented cyber incident" and pledged to support a joint investigation, with renewed attention to the risks of advanced artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Zelenskyy talks with Trump envoys amid attacks on Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said he discussed reinvigorating diplomacy with US envoys Witkoff and Kushner. The talk followed strikes by Ukraine's military on warehouses belonging to Wildberries — known as "Russia's Amazon."

stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue re

stories.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, e

stories.title: Man killed in ICE ‘encounter’ was on vacation in US with tourist visa – reports
stories.summary: Juan Jairo Coronilla Durán ran into traffic and killed by semi after federal officials approached him at Florida gas station The Mexican man who was hit and killed by a semi-truck in Florida when running away from federal immigration officials was vacationing in the US with a tourist visa, according to news reports this week. stories.title: Mykhailo Drapatyi: Ukrainians hope new commander will end army’s ‘Soviet culture’
stories.summary: Drapatyi has long been a critic of the military’s command culture, but experts warn change could take time Through a haze of smoke, a lone Ukrainian armoured vehicle suddenly burst into view. The BMP-2 accelerated towards a barricade of tyres and debris, smashing through it in seconds as protesters scattered. Behind the controls, on the chaotic streets of Mariupol on 9 May 2014, was a little-known officer named Mykhailo Drapatyi. The footage became one of the defining images of Russia’s first at

stories.title: Woman, 70, charged with murder of British expat Karen Carter in France
stories.summary: Public prosecutor for the region said suspect had been initially arrested shortly after the murder on 29 April last year A 70-year-old woman has been charged with the murder of Karen Carter, the British expat found dead in south-western France last year, according to reports. According to the Dordogne Libre and others, the woman was charged on 2 July with Carter’s murder in Trémolat, in the Dordogne, and is “under judicial supervision”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran’s foreign minister describes moment of attack that killed Khamenei
stories.summary: Abbas Araghchi says he has kept on the move since being pulled from rubble after strike on first day of war Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has described how he was pulled from the rubble of a leadership compound after the US-Israeli airstrike that killed the country’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, on the first day of the war, and has survived since by constantly moving around the capital, Tehran, by car. stories.title: India news: Opposition steps up pressure amid Cockroach protests
stories.summary: India's opposition MPs are reiterating the Cockroach protesters' demands for the education minister's resignation. Pradhan has finally addressed the protests, calling on him to step down. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Germany news: Prosecutors search Deutsche Bank headquarters
stories.summary: Officials are reportedly investigating fraudulent tax claims made by the bank's Postbank subsidiary between 2008 and 2010. Meanwhile, the story of Timmy the whale continues with new DNA results. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: France: Minister quits over pesticide bill — but is staying
stories.summary: Environment Minister Monique Barbut submitted her resignation in protest over the reintroduction of pesticides that scientists say harm human health as well as bees. But President Emmanuel Macron refused to accept it.

stories.summary: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

stories.summary: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s plans could bring the UK closer to Europe than he realises
stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? The “king of the north” is largely an unknown quantity in continental Europe and has given priority to a domestic agenda of devolving powers to region

stories.title: Norway’s national oil company’s profits double to $11.5bn amid war on Iran
stories.summary: Equinor benefits from decision to increase oil and gas production during strait of Hormuz blockades Business live – latest updates Profits at Norway’s state oil company nearly doubled to $11.5bn (£8.6bn) in the three months to the end of June as the jump in oil and gas prices caused by the war against Iran boosted earnings. stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode

stories.title: Model scout for Jeffrey Epstein found dead at his home near Paris
stories.summary: Daniel Siad faced allegations of rape and trafficking in France and appeared in more than 1,000 documents in Epstein files A modelling scout who recruited women for Jeffrey Epstein has been found dead at his home near Paris, French officials have said. Daniel Siad, 69, was under investigation by the prosecutors specialising in human trafficking in France, accused of rape and other charges. stories.title: Food price fears after Europe’s heatwave destroys estimated €2bn of crops
stories.summary: Record June temperatures expected to cut grain harvest by 9m tonnes to its lowest level since 2018 The heatwave that swept across Europe in June is estimated to have cost the continent’s grain farmers €2bn (£1.7bn) in lost revenue and destroyed 9m tonnes of crops, sparking concern about the impact on the price of food. Coceral, the European cereals and oilseeds trade association, made the estimate for the harvest of crops such as wheat, barley, maize and oats following the heatwave. Continue rea

stories.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
stories.summary: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures France recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures. The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Former CEO Matthew Wright and three other ex-employees named in criminal proceedings by Environment Agency The former chief executive of Southern Water is being prosecuted for conspiracy to defraud along with three other former employees, over allegations they manipulated sewage wastewater testing results to avoid tens of millions of pounds in fines. stories.summary: UK warehouse landlord says board unanimous in recommendation to accept ‘best and final offer’ The board of the UK warehouse landlord Segro has U-turned and said it would be willing to accept a £14bn takeover by Segro’s bigger US rival, Prologis, in a deal that would be one of the largest foreign takeovers of a UK-listed company. In the latest blow to the troubled London stock market, Segro said in a statement that its board had “unanimously concluded” it would recommend its shareholders accept w

Sign up to receive it in your inbox every Wednesday Could it be … that Donald Trump isn’t a team player? The midterm elections are less than four months away, and Republicans in the House and the Senate – hampered by a deeply unpopular president and what might be an even less popular war – are increasingly fearful of losing their

stories.title: Scottish Labour’s rivals decry ‘chancer’ Anas Sarwar for resigning to join Andy Burnham’s government
stories.summary: New prime minister plans to make Sarwar – the first senior Labour figure to call for Keir Starmer’s resignation – a peer Scottish Labour’s rivals in Holyrood have slammed Anas Sarwar’s shock decision to resign as leader to sit in Andy Burnham’s new government, calling him a “chancer” and accusing him of lying to voters. The prime minister plans to make Sarwar – who was the first senior Labour figure to call for Keir Starmer to resign – a peer and give him a ministerial post at the Department for

stories.title: Charles Gaines, whose book Pumping Iron helped to popularize bodybuilding, dies at 84
stories.summary: 1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world Charles Gaines, the author whose book and documentary Pumping Iron helped bring professional bodybuilding into the mainstream and introduce Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world, has died. He was 84. Gaines died on Tuesday, according to Greta Gaines, the author’s daughter. She did not reveal a cause. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: Party chair queries status of funds Reform leader received from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne The Conservatives have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage should have paid tax on the £5m gift he received from the crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. The party chair, Kevin Hollinrake, wrote to the tax authorities questioning whether it was a payment to compensate him for running to be an MP. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mamdani urges US government to arrest Netanyahu if Israeli PM visits US
stories.summary: New York mayor admits he does not have authority to arrest Netanyahu, adding Israeli PM is ‘architect of a horrific genocide’ Zohran Mamdani, the New York City mayor, acknowledged on Tuesday he does not have the authority to arrest Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged US federal authorities to do so if the Israeli prime minister visits the US later this year. “Benjamin Netanyahu is a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people,” Mamdani said in a video posted to so

stories.title: Can VR help you achieve your goals?
stories.summary: A Max Planck researcher has developed VR and smartphone tools that let you "meet" your future self. The science behind it is more powerful than it sounds.

stories.title: Turkey-PKK talks: Is peace finally possible?
stories.summary: A year ago, the Kurdish militant group PKK announced an end to its armed struggle. Since then, peace talks with Turkey have progressed slowly, but some observers believe they could succeed.

stories.title: Police station opens in Hitler's birth house
stories.summary: Controversy has surrounded the renovation of the building in Braunau, Austria to this day. The debate points to something larger: the continued need to remember history and confront the Nazi past.

stories.title: Will surge in German business bankruptcies hit growth?
stories.summary: Company insolvencies in Germany are rising sharply. Is this a sign of deeper economic weakness, or just a market correction that could ultimately benefit future growth?

stories.title: Can India become the next semiconductor hub after Taiwan?
stories.summary: India is investing billions to build a domestic semiconductor industry as major industrial powers seek to diversify supply chains beyond Taiwan.

stories.title: Congo: Ebola deaths increase to over 1,000
stories.summary: The fatality rate of the latest Ebola virus outbreak since mid-May is far higher than in previous outbreaks. No vaccines or treatments exist for the new strain.

stories.title: What's holding up the Gaza stabilization mission?
stories.summary: A multinational stabilization mission is supposed to ensure the reconstruction of Gaza. Without political consensus among Israel, Hamas and the Arab states, its implementation remains uncertain.

stories.title: Germany's growing interest in Africa: A win for both sides?
stories.summary: Germany is deepening its footprint across Africa, as Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul embarks on his third trip to the continent in 2026. His top-level talks will focus on security, skills development and investment.

stories.summary: Thorsten Frei, a close confidant of German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is taking over the role as parliamentary group leader for the conservative CDU/CSU bloc. The position is a powerful one.

stories.title: DW correspondent Alican Uludag released, ordered not to leave Turkey
stories.summary: Alican Uludag was freed Wednesday, a day after being taken back into custody on new charges. The arrest had come just two months after he was released pending trial in a separate case.

stories.summary: For the first time in a decade, the United Nations will soon choose a new Secretary-General. DW takes a look at the six candidates, what they stand for and the challenges they and the UN will face.

stories.title: Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopens, 9 months after heist
stories.summary: The 17th-century Apollo Gallery reopened to the public without precious jewels or other objects previously on display.

stories.title: Macron convinces environment minister to stay on
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron asked his Minister for Ecological Transition Monique Barbut to stay in her role after she resigned on Wednesday morning. “The French president refused her resignation,” government spokesperson Maud Bregeon announced at a press conference. “They agreed on three fundamental pillars that will guide her action: forests, water and environmental health … on which she agreed to remain fully invested.” Barbut’s office told French new

stories.title: EU sieht sich von Trumps neuen Generika-Zöllen nicht betroffen
stories.summary: BRÜSSEL – Die Europäische Kommission erklärte am Mittwoch, sie gehe davon aus, dass europäische Generika von den jüngsten Zöllen des US-Präsidenten Donald Trump ausgenommen seien. Sie verwies darauf, dass sich Washington im Rahmen des transatlantischen Handelsabkommens vom vergangenen Jahr zu einem Zollsatz von null verpflichtet habe. Trump hatte am Dienstag angekündigt, ab August 2028 einen Zollsatz von 100 Prozent auf Generika zu erheben, um die Arzneimittelproduktion zurück in die Verei

stories.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down

In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
stories.summary: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. stories.title: What the EU wants from Andy Burnham

EU leaders were quick to offer congratulations to Britain’s prime minister, the seventh since the 2016 Brexit referendum. The European Comm

stories.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington

stories.title: Spain wins second World Cup after beating Argentina in final
stories.summary: After a 16-year wait, Spain has claimed a second men's World Cup. Ferran Torres scored the winner in a deserved 1-0 victory over Argentina, which means Spain is now men's and women's world champions.

stories.title: Germany news: Resistance to Nazi terror commemorated
stories.summary: Monday marks 82 years since "Operation Valkyrie," the failed attempt on Adolf Hitler's life in 1944. Meanwhile, Foreign Minister Wadephul is heading to Africa on official business.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's new prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Burnham, the former Manchester mayor, has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: UN chief Guterres to visit Cyprus in bid to revive peace talks
stories.summary: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres will visit Cyprus next week in what is seen as an effort to kickstart the stalled peace process. Why now? What is known about the plan? And what might be different this time round?

stories.title: AliExpress hit with record EU fine
stories.summary: The Chinese retailer has become the latest major online marketplace to face penalties under the EU’s Digital Services Act.

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
stories.summary: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, wildfires in Europe, ICE in Maine and the World Cup semi-finals – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: New footage shows assassination attempt on Ukrainian businessman in Monaco – video
stories.summary: Ukraine's prosecutor general, Ruslan Kravchenko, said investigators have recovered key evidence in the case of the attempted assassination of the Ukrainian businessman Vadym Iermolaiev in Monaco. Specialists from Ukraine's security services restored a surveillance-camera recording that suspects had allegedly attempted to destroy. According to the prosecutor general, the surveillance camera had been installed near the crime scene to obtain confirmation that the alleged contract killing had been c

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

stories.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
stories.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

stories.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
stories.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

stories.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
stories.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

stories.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
stories.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

stories.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
stories.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

stories.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
stories.summary: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

stories.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
stories.summary: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

stories.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
stories.summary: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

stories.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
stories.summary: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix
